message:
feat(rch-common): add Icons utility with Unicode/ASCII fallbacks

Implement the Icons struct in rch-common/src/ui/icons.rs providing
Unicode icons with automatic ASCII fallbacks for terminals that don't
support Unicode (CI environments, older terminals, etc.).

Icon categories:
- Status indicators: check, cross, warning, info
- Worker status: healthy, degraded, unreachable, draining, disabled
- Arrows: right, left, up, down
- List/bullet: bullet, bullet_hollow, tree_branch, tree_end,
  tree_vertical, tree_horizontal
- Activity: worker, compile, transfer, clock, gear
- Slot visualization: slot_filled, slot_empty, slot_partial
- Progress bar: progress_filled, progress_empty, progress_head
- Miscellaneous: lightning, lightbulb, lock, hourglass, spinner_frames

All icon methods take an OutputContext parameter to determine whether
to return Unicode characters or ASCII fallbacks. This ensures consistent
output across different terminal environments.

Example usage:
  let ctx = OutputContext::detect();
  eprintln!("{} Build successful", Icons::check(ctx));

Files:
- CREATE: rch-common/src/ui/icons.rs
- MODIFY: rch-common/src/ui/mod.rs (add icons module, update docs)
- MODIFY: rch-common/src/lib.rs (add Icons re-export)

Tests: 6 unit tests covering Unicode/ASCII fallbacks, spinner frames,
hook/machine mode, and distinct status icons

Closes bd-17dh

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
